args:
Validator Name: CrypTech
Location: Ukraine
Infrastructure: Personal datacenter + cloud

CrypTech is a non-custodial validator operations and staking infrastructure provider focused on reliable, secure, and cost-efficient Proof-of-Stake operations.

We currently operate and support validator infrastructure across IOTA, ZetaChain, Zilliqa, Somnia, Mitosis, and Monad. Part of our work is delivered as white-label validator operations for programs associated with Lunar Strategy and Hacken. CrypTech is responsible for operational support, including node infrastructure, upgrades, monitoring, and incident response.

Total AuM: ~$4.8M across all networks.

Digital presence:
- Website: https://cryptech.space
- X/Twitter: https://x.com/CryptechNodes
- GitHub: https://github.com/dvjromashkin
- Discord: romashkin_cryptech

Contact:
- Email: nodes@cryptech.com.ua

Why Gno.land:
We have followed Gno.land since its early testnets and are interested in supporting a developer-focused application-chain stack that takes a different approach from the Cosmos SDK ecosystem. Validator operations are our core focus, and we want to contribute through reliable infrastructure and long-term testnet participation.

Contributions & plans:
For Gno Topaz, we already operate a synchronized node. We are ready to support the ecosystem with infrastructure services for operators and the community, including snapshots, public RPC/API endpoints, network and peer-status monitoring, lightweight explorer/validator visibility tools, node/RPC health dashboards, and practical notes and guides for new validators.

args:
## Network Support

We are currently validating on multiple blockchain ecosystems including Atomone, Supra, Dymension, Aztec, Somnia, Avail, SEDA, Redbelly, SQD, and several other Cosmos, EVM, and Substrate networks, reflecting our established presence and trusted reputation in the validator ecosystem as a professional security audited company.

## Digital Presence & Contact
 
Website: www.tecnodes.network 

X (Twitter): https://x.com/tecnodesnetwork 

Telegram: tecnodes_network 

Discord: tecnodes

## Why gno.land 

We are deeply interested in validating on gno.land due to its innovative smart contract platform and unique approach to blockchain development. Gno.land's emphasis on simplicity, security, and developer experience aligns perfectly with our expertise in blockchain security auditing. The platform's potential to revolutionize smart contract development through its Go-based programming model represents a significant advancement in the space that we want to actively support.

## Contributions 

As an active contribution to the gno.land ecosystem, we are currently running a Test13 & have participated in Test6, Test7, Test9 and Test11 to support network stability and testing. We have also developed GNO explorer with Adena wallet integration. Moving forward, we plan to contribute to our highest capacity by leveraging our security audit expertise to help identify potential vulnerabilities furthermore we would provide RPC, API & snapshots services, participate in governance decisions, and provide technical feedback to improve the platform's security posture. Our experience as professional validator positions us uniquely to contribute meaningful insights for the network's long-term security and decentralization goals. Note: for testnet we are using data-center, however for mainnet we'll use on-prem hosting.

StakeUp is a staking provider and validator team operating blockchain infrastructure since 2021. We focus on stable validator operations, network participation, infrastructure support, and long-term ecosystem support. 

## Network
We currently operate validators across more than 30 blockchain networks, including: 
- Atomone 
- Celestia 
- Canton
- Dymension
- Starknet
- Near
- Shentu
- Doravota
- Espresso
- Nym
- Provenance
- Haqq
- Pocket
- Bitway,
- Warden and others. 

## Links: 
- Website: https://stakeup.tech
- X (Twitter): https://x.com/stakeup_tech

## Contact Details: 
- Email: contact@stakeup.tech
- Telegram: https://t.me/stakeup_team
- Discord: landerosua

## Why We're Interested in Validating on gno.land: 
We have been participating in blockchain ecosystems and operating validators for several years. gno.land is an interesting ecosystem, and we would like to contribute by running reliable infrastructure and supporting the network as it develops. 

## Contributions: 
- Participation in testnets and network testing, 
- Providing public services such as RPC, API, and snapshots, 
- Developing tools and dashboards, 
- Supporting network upgrades and validator operations.

args:
## Networks We Are Currently Validating & AuM:

Perfect Nodes provides infrastructure and validator services across multiple blockchain ecosystems. We currently operate validators and node infrastructure for networks including: 
- Provenance: 45M HASH 
- Starknet: 2.84M STRK
- Espresso: 2M ESP
- DoraVota: 4M DORA
- Ika: 64M IKA
- Haqq: 8M ISLM
- Uptick: 2M UPTICK
- Canton and other

## Links to Our Digital Presence:

- Website: https://perfect-nodes.com
- X (Twitter): https://x.com/perfect_nodes

## Contact Details:

- Telegram: https://t.me/perfect_nodes
- Discord: katekrasv

## Why We Are Interested in Validating on gno.land:

Perfect-nodes has been validating across multiple networks for several years, and we usually prefer to get involved early in strong ecosystems and stay in for the long term. gno.land stands out to us because of its team and the direction the project is taking, which makes it a good fit.

Our focus is simple — run a stable and reliable validator and contribute to the network beyond block production. This includes providing public RPC and snapshots, participating in governance, and supporting other validators and users where we can.

We plan to stay long-term and keep a reliable validator running as gno.land grows.

## Contributions to gno.land:

Testnet participation
Public RPC, API, and snapshot services
Development operational tools
Guides
Validator operations and upgrade support

# Validator Name: OnNode

OnNode is a professional validator operator focused on secure node operations, fast snapshots, and practical infrastructure support across multiple blockchain networks.

# Networks We Are Currently Validating:

- Starknet: https://dashboard.endur.fi/validator/0x04a044a2641974655f8429a04f3e7300e888f93bf7e13d95438ba47d1830a402
- Near: https://near-staking.com/validator/onnode.pool.near
- Espresso: https://espressonodes.com/epoch?network=mainnet

Total AuM: $202K

# Links to Our Digital Presence:

- Website: https://onnode.org/
- Documentation: https://onnode.org/docs/intro
- X/Twitter: https://x.com/onnode_staking
- GitHub: https://github.com/onnode-ops

# Contact Details:

- Email: huannv@onnode.org
- Telegram: onnode
- Discord: onnode

# Why We Are Interested in Validating on gno.land:

We are excited about gno.land’s developer-centric approach and its use of Gno — a Go-based smart contract language. Combined with transparent on-chain governance, we see strong potential to support the network as a reliable validator while contributing practical infrastructure and documentation.

# Contributions & Plans:

OnNode provides battle-tested infrastructure including fast snapshots, comprehensive node operation guides, high-performance RPC endpoints, and 24/7 technical support.

For gno.land, we plan to:

- Operate a reliable validator with high uptime
- Launch and maintain fast snapshots
- Create practical guides for node operators
- Provide public RPC endpoints and monitoring tools
- Assist new validators with onboarding and best practices
- We are committed to supporting gno.land’s growth and its developer community.

files.name: proposal.gno
files.body:
package main

import (
	"gno.land/r/gov/dao"
	valv3 "gno.land/r/sys/validators/v3"
)

func main(cur realm) {
	r := valv3.NewValidatorProposalRequest(
		cross(cur),
		[]valv3.ValoperChange{
			valv3.NewValoperChange(address("g14m0n559xdj00qwvp6ck0xesprrq26kgprgjt4j"), 5),
			valv3.NewValoperChange(address("g1ppvaadzu0smzqyrgvw73hjdrhvlu43l7g7tw3f"), 5),
			valv3.NewValoperChange(address("g1pskxvnzkawh4hze3tdn8u7jj59pcs4ukku7fv5"), 5),
			valv3.NewValoperChange(address("g1ujtcuq6xuttkjyhcxse07gp6lzu4h6gmx978yc"), 5),
			valv3.NewValoperChange(address("g1l3fmz59rw8l6h29hslxt6gtna3th8s7m5vl88d"), 5),
			valv3.NewValoperChange(address("g1u7g4cejjqsm44nha34hzpzu6df6a7egc9rpqw5"), 5),
			valv3.NewValoperChange(address("g1y4l44w8fwl8tj473y49l3ay3k2nad0pdhchn3d"), 5),
			valv3.NewValoperChange(address("g1etf3rd7dqghksj8r5yunkgmvpupdfszdjhuzju"), 5),
			valv3.NewValoperChange(address("g1e3vld469ekks28mhtd4aqua3mdaz64qgdtz29k"), 5),
			valv3.NewValoperChange(address("g1lyt8fg7e987jgh7kxa3fn8zlcx295kdzuj7d8d"), 5),
		},
		"Add 10 validator(s) to the valset",
		"Batch-add 10 operator(s) to the active valset with voting power 5.",
	)
	pid := dao.MustCreateProposal(cross(cur), r)
	dao.MustVoteOnProposalSimple(cross(cur), int64(pid), "YES")
	dao.ExecuteProposal(cross(cur), pid)
}
